features.title: Frontend project recovery init
features.description: Upgrade the root init.sh from harness-only verification into the frontend recovery contract for a fresh checkout.
acceptance: Root ./init.sh still runs the AI Agent Harness verification layer.
Root ./init.sh installs or verifies npm dependencies from the lockfile.
Root ./init.sh runs TypeScript and Jest verification, and build verification when dependencies are available.
The script emits clear setup, verification, and success or failure logs and exits non-zero on failures.

features.title: Auth session contract coverage
features.description: Make frontend auth storage, protected routing, refresh retry, OAuth callback, and logout behavior durably verified.
acceptance: Protected routes redirect unauthenticated users to /login.
The HTTP client attaches bearer tokens and retries after a successful refresh.
Refresh failure clears stored auth state and returns the user to login.
Google OAuth callback handling matches the backend callback response contract.

features.title: Upload Ask UI contract coverage
features.description: Add durable verification for Upload & Ask document upload, question asking, citations, and session history UI behavior.
acceptance: Multipart upload sends Authorization and FormData without forcing JSON content headers.
Document status, failure reason, session, query log, answer, latency, and citation fields match backend JSON contracts.
Ask and history errors are surfaced without corrupting existing state.
Tests use mocked API responses and do not require a live backend by default.

features.title: Cross repository API contract alignment
features.description: Keep frontend request and response types aligned with the sibling backend repository.
acceptance: The frontend records the backend repository path and shared API surfaces in durable docs or SPEC notes.
Contract-sensitive JSON fields such as refreshToken, durationMs, tokenUsage, sessionId, and partial_summary are documented.
A verification step or fixture catches drift for routes used by the frontend.
The backend sibling repository receives matching contract notes or feature entries.
